sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Current state: center tile is green, all others are gray. The green bar on the right edge has shrunk from the full height (rows 0-63) to rows 12-63 (length 52). This suggests the green bar is a "remaining moves" counter that decreases with each click.
The initial right grid had a checkerboard pattern (white and gray tiles). After many clicks, all tiles became gray. The white tiles may have been "used up" or the grid reset.
Now I need to figure out the goal. The left grid has purple dots in a cross/diamond pattern. The right grid needs to match this. But the right grid is 3x3, and the pattern on the left is 4 dots (top, bottom, left, right centers).
Current right grid:
```
g  g  g
g  N  g
g  g  g
```
Target pattern (from left grid - cross shape):
```
g  N  g
N  g  N
g  N  g
```
So I need to click the 4 edge-center tiles (top, bottom, left, right) to turn them green, while keeping the center gray. But the center is currently green, so I need to click it to turn it back to gray, then click the 4 edge tiles.
Wait, but the green bar is decreasing. If I've used up 12 moves already (64 - 52 = 12), and I need 5 more clicks (center + 4 edges), that's 17 total which should be fine if the bar started at 64.
Let me proceed with the plan: click center to un-green, then click the 4 edge tiles.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Green bar on right edge is a move counter (started at 64, now at 52). Center tile is green, all others gray. Goal: match left grid's cross pattern on right grid (4 edge-centers green, center and corners gray).
Plan: Click center to turn gray, then click 4 edge-center tiles to turn green.

    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30},   # top-center: gray -> green
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25},   # left-center: gray -> green
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35},   # right-center: gray -> green
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30},   # bottom-center: gray -> green
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
